name: SPIR-V
summary: Khronos Group's portable GPU intermediate representation. Binary SSA IR consumed by Vulkan, OpenCL, Mesa, and most modern GPU drivers. Lets frontend compilers (HLSL → SPIR-V via DXC, GLSL → SPIR-V via glslang, Slang, Metal Shading Language) target one IR rather than per-driver IRs.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
SPIR-V is the Khronos Group's portable GPU intermediate representation. A binary SSA IR designed for GPU shaders and compute kernels, SPIR-V is consumed by Vulkan, OpenCL, Mesa drivers, and most modern GPU runtimes. Frontend compilers (HLSL → SPIR-V via DXC, GLSL → SPIR-V via glslang, Slang, Metal Shading Language → SPIR-V via various translators) target this single IR rather than per-driver IRs.

The payoff: one SPIR-V module runs across NVIDIA, AMD, Intel, ARM Mali, and Apple GPU drivers. The driver lowers SPIR-V to the vendor's native ISA at runtime. Application code is GPU-vendor-agnostic at the IR level.

sections.title: Design
sections.content:
SPIR-V is binary, SSA-form, and typed at instruction granularity. The instruction set is broad — graphics shaders, compute kernels, ray-tracing shaders, mesh shaders all share the same IR with capability flags marking which features a module uses.

The binary format is compact and validatable: drivers verify SPIR-V on load to ensure type-safety, control-flow validity, and capability compatibility before lowering to native code. This is the GPU analogue of WebAssembly's validation model — the IR is portable and verified before it runs.
